title: lophodium recurvum
intro: Lophodium recurvum, also known as the club moss, is a type of plant that belongs to the genus Lycopodiopsida and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lophodium recurvum include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tea or tincture, and it is also sometimes dried and used in potpourri blends due to its earthy, moss-like fragrance.
